Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is impaired (elevated serum creatinine), and urine shows significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and bilirubinuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is low, possibly due to anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney disease with tubular dysfunction; urinary tract infection is less likely (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend further nephrological evaluation and correlation with clinical findings.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
